checks.reason: Recursive force-delete on project or system paths is irreversible.
checks.fix: Scope rm -rf to explicit temp dirs. Never run on . or $PROJECT_ROOT.

checks.description: Pipe to shell interpreter (curl|bash, wget|sh, etc.)
checks.reason: Fetching and executing remote code is the most common supply chain attack vector.
checks.fix: Download first, inspect hash, then execute. Never pipe remote content to a shell.

checks.reason: World-writable files/directories allow any user or process to overwrite them.
checks.fix: Use least-privilege permissions: 755 for executables, 644 for files.

checks.reason: sudo bash/sh opens a full root shell — all subsequent commands run as root.
checks.fix: Use sudo only with specific, named commands. Never sudo into an interactive shell.

checks.description: eval with variable or subshell content
checks.reason: eval of dynamic content enables shell injection if any input is user-controlled.
checks.fix: Replace eval with direct function calls or array-based command construction.

checks.description: SQL DROP TABLE / DROP DATABASE in script
checks.reason: Irreversible database destruction. Should never be in an auto-run script.
checks.fix: Move destructive SQL to a manually-triggered migration with backup gate.

checks.description: Unquoted variable used in command (shellcheck SC2086)
checks.reason: Unquoted variables cause word splitting — $DIR could expand to multiple paths.
checks.fix: Always double-quote variables: rm "$FILE" not rm $FILE.

checks.description: set -e missing in script — errors may be silently ignored
checks.reason: Without set -e, a failed command does not stop the script.
checks.fix: Add 'set -euo pipefail' after the shebang line.

checks.reason: Skipping TLS certificate verification enables MITM attacks.
checks.fix: Remove -k. Fix the actual certificate issue instead.

checks.reason: Force-push overwrites remote history and can destroy shared work permanently.
checks.fix: Use --force-with-lease if you must, and only after human review.
